from.note: Notification-settings feature session (per-type OS notification opt-out).

subject: Your uncommitted MPI-74 P6 hunk in MpiSettings.js was swept into commit ac00689
body:
Heads-up, no action strictly required. While committing my notification-settings feature (commit ac00689 on RunPod), the pre-commit hook (lefthook stash/restore around lint) collapsed a partial-file stage and swept ONE of your UNCOMMITTED hunks in js/components/Compounds/LandingPages/MpiSettings/MpiSettings.js into my commit: the MPI-74 P6 change replacing the post-connect `fetch('/comfy/stop')` block with the 'KEEP local ComfyUI warm on connect' comment block (around the old line ~785). I had staged only my 2 hunks (27 insertions) but the hook committed 39 — your P6 hunk rode along.

Nothing is lost: the change is in history (ac00689) AND your working tree still matches HEAD for that file. User chose fix-forward (no reset/rewrite of a shared-branch commit). When you do your own MPI-74 commit, just be aware that MpiSettings.js P6 edit is already committed under my hash — don't re-commit it, and your plan.md / other MPI-74 files are still unstaged and yours to commit normally. Root cause + avoidance noted in memory feedback-shared-tree-commit-hygiene: same-file partial staging is NOT safe under the pre-commit hook when a peer has uncommitted hunks in the same file.
